memes.label: A Song for Occupations — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'A Song for Occupations' — Whitman's comprehensive celebration of labor, trade, and the dignity of all honest work in democratic America.

memes.label: A Song of Joys — Analysis
memes.description: A literary analysis of Whitman's 'A Song of Joys' (BOOK XI) — an exuberant catalogue of human pleasures spanning body, mind, labor, love, and cosmic wonder.

memes.label: Abraham Lincoln
memes.description: 16th President of the United States, assassinated in 1865; the central figure of Whitman's two great elegies "When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloom'd" and "O Captain! My Captain!"

memes.label: Alabama
memes.description: A Deep South state referenced across 5 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues of cotton fields and American landscapes as a marker of the plantation South and its transformation.

memes.label: Alfred Tennyson
memes.description: British Poet Laureate (1809–1892), contemporary of Whitman and the most prominent English-language poet of the Victorian era, referenced across 4 corpus passages in Leaves of Grass.

memes.label: America
memes.description: America in Leaves of Grass is both the literal nation and a cosmic ideal — the democratic experiment, the land of unprecedented diversity, and the stage for humanity's spiritual unfolding.

memes.label: American Civil War
memes.description: The 1861–1865 conflict that divided the United States and profoundly transformed Walt Whitman's life and poetry, inspiring his hospital volunteer work and his great elegies.

memes.label: Arkansas
memes.description: A Southern state referenced across 6 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues of rivers, settlements, and American diversity as a marker of the lower Mississippi region.

memes.label: Beauty
memes.description: The quality of sensory and spiritual excellence that Whitman finds in the human body, the natural world, and the democratic multitude — a beauty inseparable from truth and power.

memes.label: Blacksmith
memes.description: The Blacksmith in Leaves of Grass represents the dignity of skilled manual labor in Whitman's democratic celebration of all honest trades.

memes.label: Boatman
memes.description: The Boatman in Leaves of Grass is an archetypal American worker — celebrated by Whitman as a figure of democratic dignity, skilled labor, and essential contribution to the national whole.

memes.label: The Body
memes.description: In Leaves of Grass, the body is celebrated as sacred, equal to the soul, and the vessel through which all human experience — love, labor, mortality — is realized.

memes.label: Brooklyn
memes.description: Brooklyn in Leaves of Grass is the young Whitman's home, the site of the ferry crossing that became his great meditation on connection across time and space.

memes.label: California
memes.description: California in Leaves of Grass is the far western frontier — the Golden State of miners, pioneers, and the Pacific horizon of American destiny.

memes.label: Canada
memes.description: Canada in Leaves of Grass represents the northern neighbor — vast, wild, and part of the continental sweep of Whitman's democratic vision.

memes.label: Captain
memes.description: The Captain in Leaves of Grass represents the dignity of skilled manual labor in Whitman's democratic celebration of all honest trades.

memes.label: Carpenter
memes.description: The Carpenter in Leaves of Grass represents the dignity of skilled manual labor in Whitman's democratic celebration of all honest trades.

memes.label: Chicago
memes.description: Chicago in Leaves of Grass is the Midwestern hub — the railroad nexus, the prairie metropolis, the city where East meets West in Whitman's American catalogue.

memes.label: Colorado
memes.description: The Rocky Mountain state referenced in 8 corpus passages across Leaves of Grass, celebrated for its canyons, winds, mountain men, and symbolic role as the high-altitude heart of the American West.

memes.label: Columbia
memes.description: Poetic name for America and the Pacific Northwest's great river, referenced across 9 corpus passages in Leaves of Grass as a symbol of the continent's farthest reach and national destiny.

memes.label: Comrade
memes.description: Comradeship in Leaves of Grass is the passionate bond between equals — Whitman's "adhesiveness" — the emotional foundation of democratic society and a love that rivals romantic attachment.

memes.label: Crossing Brooklyn Ferry — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'Crossing Brooklyn Ferry' — Whitman's meditation on time, connection, and the shared experience that unites past, present, and future travelers.

memes.label: Dakota
memes.description: The vast northern Plains territory referenced across 5 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's poetry as "Far Dakota" — a remote, mythic landscape of canyons, woods, and the drama of American expansion.

memes.label: Dante Alighieri
memes.description: Italian poet (c. 1265–1321), author of the Divine Comedy, referenced across 3 corpus passages in Leaves of Grass as a literary precursor and spiritual journey archetype.

memes.label: Death
memes.description: In Leaves of Grass, death is not an ending but a transformation — Whitman presents it as beautiful, indifferent, and inseparable from life and rebirth.

memes.label: Democracy
memes.description: The central political and spiritual concept of Whitman's poetry — democracy as both a system of government and a spiritual vision of human equality, inclusivity, and comradeship.

memes.label: The Earth
memes.description: The Earth in Leaves of Grass is the democratic ground of being — the physical planet that all people share, the source of all life, and the stage for humanity's unfolding drama.

relations.label: A Song of the Rolling Earth

memes.label: Edgar Allan Poe
memes.description: American poet and writer (1809–1849), acknowledged by Whitman as a significant literary predecessor whose "melancholy" genius influenced the American poetic tradition.

memes.label: Equality
memes.description: Equality as the foundational democratic principle in Leaves of Grass — not merely political but cosmic, extending to all beings and things.

memes.label: Faith
memes.description: Faith in Leaves of Grass is not doctrinal religion but democratic trust — faith in comrades, in the body, in the future of America, and in the generative power of nature itself, expressed across 3+ corpus passages.

memes.label: Farmer
memes.description: The Farmer in Leaves of Grass is an archetypal American worker — celebrated by Whitman as a figure of democratic dignity, skilled labor, and essential contribution to the national whole.

memes.label: Florida
memes.description: The southeastern peninsula state referenced in 8 corpus passages across Leaves of Grass, evoked through its green landscapes, transparent lakes, orange groves, and wintering coasts.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: Freedom in Leaves of Grass is both political and existential — the emancipation of slaves, the liberty of democratic citizens, and the soul's liberation from all that constrains its expansion.

memes.label: George Washington
memes.description: First President of the United States (1732–1799), commander of the Continental Army, and foundational figure in American national identity, referenced across 5+ corpus passages in Leaves of Grass.

memes.label: Georgia
memes.description: The southeastern state referenced in 8 corpus passages across Leaves of Grass, associated with cotton cultivation, coastal landscapes, and Whitman's catalogues of American southern places.

memes.label: God
memes.description: God in Leaves of Grass is radically immanent — found in every person, every object, every moment — not a distant judge but the divinity inherent in all existence, most fully realized in the self.

memes.label: Grass
memes.description: Grass in Leaves of Grass is the central symbol — democratic, ever-renewing, growing alike from graves and fertile soil, the flag of death's continuity and nature's egalitarian abundance.

relations.label: A Song of the Rolling Earth

memes.label: Health
memes.description: Whitman's celebration of physical and spiritual health as a democratic ideal — the sound body as the foundation of the sound self, the sound polity, and the sound cosmos.

memes.label: Homer
memes.description: Legendary ancient Greek epic poet (c. 8th century BCE), author of the Iliad and Odyssey, referenced by Whitman as the archetypal bard whose epic vision prefigures the democratic poet's own ambition.

memes.label: Hunter
memes.description: The Hunter in Leaves of Grass is an archetypal American worker — celebrated by Whitman as a figure of democratic dignity, skilled labor, and essential contribution to the national whole.

memes.label: I Sing the Body Electric
memes.description: Analysis of 'I Sing the Body Electric' — Whitman's great celebration of the physical body as sacred, democratic, and inseparable from the soul.

memes.label: Identity
memes.description: Central Whitman concept — the paradox of individual identity fusing with cosmic unity, the "knit of identity" that binds all beings while preserving distinction.

memes.label: Illinois
memes.description: A central Midwestern state referenced in 8 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues as a quintessential prairie landscape and heartland of the democratic republic.

memes.label: The Immigrant
memes.description: The Immigrant in Leaves of Grass is the renewing force of American democracy — arriving continuously, bringing diversity and energy, and embodying the nation's identity as a haven for all the world's peoples.

memes.label: Immortality
memes.description: Immortality in Leaves of Grass is not a distant promise but a present reality — the soul is immortal now, death is a passage not an end, and every moment participates in eternity.

memes.label: Indiana
memes.description: A central Midwestern state referenced across 6 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's pivotal catalogues as quintessential heartland — the fertile interior that sustains the democratic republic.

memes.label: Iowa
memes.description: A Midwestern prairie state referenced across 4 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's foundational catalogues as part of the fertile interior — the agricultural heartland that sustains the democratic republic.

memes.label: Jesus Christ
memes.description: Central figure of Christianity, referenced by Whitman in Leaves of Grass as a spiritual archetype whose democratic compassion and immanent divinity the poet absorbs into his own vision of sacred democracy.

memes.label: Journey
memes.description: The journey as spiritual metaphor in Leaves of Grass — the open road as condition of freedom, movement as mode of knowing, pilgrimage without fixed destination.

memes.label: Joy
memes.description: Joy in Leaves of Grass is not mere happiness but cosmic exultation — the ecstatic affirmation of existence in all its forms, a democratic delight that excludes nothing and no one.

memes.label: Kansas
memes.description: A Great Plains state referenced across 5 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues as the geographic center of the continent — "from the centre from Kansas" — the prairie heart of the democratic republic.

memes.label: Kentucky
memes.description: A border state referenced across 5 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues of agriculture and American types as a landscape of farms, corn-fields, and the contested ground between North and South.

memes.label: Labor
memes.description: Whitman's celebration of physical work and the working person as the foundation of democratic society and a source of dignity, identity, and poetry.

memes.label: Liberty
memes.description: Whitman's fusion of personal and political liberty — the "uncompromising liberty and equality" that animates both the individual soul and the American experiment.

memes.label: Life
memes.description: The animating force that Whitman celebrates throughout Leaves of Grass — physical, sensual, and ever-renewing.

memes.label: Love
memes.description: Love in Leaves of Grass spans romantic, comradely, democratic, and cosmic dimensions — the binding force that connects individuals to each other and to the universe.

memes.label: Mannahatta
memes.description: "Mannahatta — Whitman's name for Manhattan — is the urban heart of Leaves of Grass: the democratic city where all races, trades, and nations mix, and the poet's own beloved home."

memes.label: Mariner
memes.description: The Mariner in Leaves of Grass is an archetypal American worker — celebrated by Whitman as a figure of democratic dignity, skilled labor, and essential contribution to the national whole.

memes.label: The Mechanic
memes.description: The Mechanic in Leaves of Grass is the archetypal American worker — skilled, dignified, and central to Whitman's democratic vision where manual labor is celebrated as fully as intellectual or artistic achievement.

memes.label: Miner
memes.description: The Miner in Leaves of Grass represents the dignity of skilled manual labor in Whitman's democratic celebration of all honest trades.

memes.label: The Mississippi
memes.description: The Mississippi River in Leaves of Grass is the spinal river of the American continent — the lifeblood connecting North and South, East and West.

memes.label: Missouri
memes.description: A central state and great river of the American interior, Missouri appears throughout Whitman's catalogues as a symbol of westward expansion, continental vastness, and the democratic mixing of peoples.

memes.label: The Mother
memes.description: The Mother in Leaves of Grass is the generative principle — the source of all life, the perfect nurturer, and a figure of cosmic significance who rivals and complements the poet's expansive self.

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: Nature as Whitman's all-encompassing physical and spiritual reality — the material world that grounds his democratic vision, source of his most expansive imagery and the ultimate teacher of the self.

memes.label: New England
memes.description: New England in Leaves of Grass is the nation's historical seed — the Puritan, Transcendentalist, and literary homeland from which the American experiment grew.

memes.label: Niagara
memes.description: Niagara Falls in Leaves of Grass is the sublime force of American nature — thunderous, boundary-crossing, and emblematic of the continent's primal power.

memes.label: Night
memes.description: Night in Leaves of Grass is the time of mystery, soul-journey, erotic union, and cosmic vision — not the absence of day but a positive realm of deeper knowing and intimate connection.

memes.label: O Captain! My Captain!
memes.description: Analysis of 'O Captain! My Captain!' — Whitman's most famous poem, the popular elegy for Lincoln using the extended metaphor of ship-as-nation and captain-as-martyred-leader.

memes.label: Ohio
memes.description: A Midwestern state and river central to Whitman's poetic geography of America, appearing frequently in his catalogic passages as a symbol of the fertile, expanding republic.

memes.label: Oregon
memes.description: The Pacific Northwest state referenced across 7 corpus passages in Leaves of Grass, embodying the farthest western reach, pioneer trails, and Whitman's vision of continental completion.

memes.label: Osceola
memes.description: Seminole leader (1804–1838) who led resistance to forced removal, a single name in Leaves of Grass that evokes the Native American presence in Whitman's American panorama.

memes.label: Our Old Feuillage — Analysis
memes.description: A literary analysis of Whitman's 'Our Old Feuillage' (BOOK X) — a panoramic celebration of American diversity using the metaphor of perennial foliage to image national unity through multiplicity.

memes.label: Out of the Cradle Endlessly Rocking
memes.description: "Analysis of 'Out of the Cradle Endlessly Rocking' — Whitman's elegy of childhood loss, the mockingbird's song, and the birth of the poet from the sea's whispered word: death."

memes.label: Passage to India — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'Passage to India' — Whitman's great poem of global connection, celebrating the Suez Canal, transatlantic cable, and the soul's voyage beyond physical boundaries.

memes.label: Paumanok
memes.description: Paumanok — the indigenous name for Long Island — is Whitman's birthplace, the fish-shaped starting point of his poetic journey and the origin myth of his self.

memes.label: Peace
memes.description: The condition of tranquility and reconciliation that Whitman envisions for the individual soul, the war-torn nation, and the cosmos — a peace that encompasses rather than excludes conflict.

memes.label: Pennsylvania
memes.description: A foundational Mid-Atlantic state referenced across 6 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues of American products and peoples as a keystone of the industrial and agricultural republic.

memes.label: The Pioneer
memes.description: The Pioneer in Leaves of Grass is the archetypal American — pushing westward, breaking new ground, embodying the restless democratic energy that Whitman celebrates as the nation's defining spirit.

memes.label: Pioneers
memes.description: The pioneer in Whitman's Leaves of Grass embodies democratic momentum — the westward-moving American who transforms wilderness into civilization, celebrated in the rousing refrain "Pioneers! O Pioneers!" across 10+ corpus passages.

memes.label: Pioneers! O Pioneers!
memes.description: Analysis of 'Pioneers! O Pioneers!' — Whitman's marching song of westward expansion and democratic progress, driven by its insistent refrain.

memes.label: The Prairies
memes.description: The Prairies in Leaves of Grass represent the vast American interior — open, wild, democratic, and charged with spiritual significance as the nation's expanding frontier.

memes.label: Prayer of Columbus — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'Prayer of Columbus' — a dramatic monologue of the aged, shipwrecked Columbus pouring out his soul to God, a figure of heroic failure and spiritual exaltation.

memes.label: "Front Matter — Leaves of Grass"
memes.description: "Front Matter — Leaves of Grass"

memes.label: Proud Music of the Storm — Analysis
memes.description: A late-career Whitman poem exploring music as the bridge between nature and the soul, storm and symphony, the material world and spiritual transcendence.

memes.label: Ralph Waldo Emerson
memes.description: American essayist, philosopher, and transcendentalist (1803–1882), Whitman's most important intellectual mentor, referenced in Leaves of Grass alongside other literary giants.

memes.label: Sailor
memes.description: The Sailor in Leaves of Grass is an archetypal American worker — celebrated by Whitman as a figure of democratic dignity, skilled labor, and essential contribution to the national whole.

memes.label: Salut au Monde! — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'Salut au Monde!' — Whitman's global embrace, a poetic voyage around the world that catalogs nations, peoples, and landscapes in democratic celebration.

memes.label: The Sea
memes.description: The Sea in Leaves of Grass is the cosmic mother, the democratic leveler, and the symbol of infinite possibility — Whitman's imagination repeatedly returns to its shores and depths.

memes.label: The Self
memes.description: The Self in Leaves of Grass is Whitman's central conceit — an expansive, cosmic "I" that contains multitudes, embracing contradictions and identifying with all humanity, nature, and the universe.

memes.label: The Soldier
memes.description: The Soldier in Leaves of Grass is the democratic hero — the common volunteer who suffers, fights, and dies for the Union, transformed by Whitman's hospital nursing into a figure of sacred sacrifice.

memes.label: Song/Music
memes.description: Song and music as the central metaphor for poetic creation in Leaves of Grass — the poet as singer, the poem as song, the cosmos as music.

memes.label: Song of Myself — Analysis
memes.description: A comprehensive analysis of Song of Myself, Whitman's longest and most celebrated poem — a cosmic journey of the self through body, soul, nature, and democracy.

memes.label: Song of the Answerer — Analysis
memes.description: A literary analysis of Whitman's 'Song of the Answerer' (BOOK IX) — a meditation on the role of the poet as cosmic answerer, translating the signs of the world for humanity.

memes.label: Song of the Broad-Axe — Analysis
memes.description: One of Whitman's major long poems from the 1856 edition, using the axe as a central symbol for democracy, labor, and the building of civilization across continents and epochs.

memes.label: Song of the Exposition — Analysis
memes.description: A literary analysis of Whitman's 'Song of the Exposition' (BOOK XIII) — a poem of American material progress, celebrating industry, agriculture, and the democratic spirit of the nation's productive power.

memes.label: Song of the Open Road — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'Song of the Open Road' — the great poem of journey, freedom, and the open air as spiritual condition and democratic metaphor.

memes.label: Song of the Redwood-Tree — Analysis
memes.description: A literary analysis of Whitman's 'Song of the Redwood-Tree' (BOOK XIV) — a death-song for a dying California redwood that becomes a meditation on westward expansion, ecological loss, and American destiny.

memes.label: The Soul
memes.description: The immortal spiritual essence at the heart of Leaves of Grass — companion to the body, vehicle of transcendent experience, and the divine core of every individual.

memes.label: Spirit
memes.description: The animating, immaterial dimension of existence in Whitman's cosmos — neither opposed to the body nor separate from it, but immanent within the physical world and accessible through sensory experience.

memes.label: Starting from Paumanok — Analysis
memes.description: "Analysis of 'Starting from Paumanok' — the opening poem that establishes Whitman's themes: the self, the body, the soul, America, democracy, and the poet's mission."

memes.label: Sun
memes.description: Central symbol in Whitman's Leaves of Grass — the sun as generative force, democratic light, and cosmic companion, appearing across 13+ passages in the corpus.

memes.label: Tennessee
memes.description: A Southern state and river system referenced across 8 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's catalogues of American geography and agricultural abundance as a borderland of the Upper South.

memes.label: Texas
memes.description: The vast southwestern state referenced across 10 corpus passages, embodying frontier expansiveness, the cotton economy, and Whitman's vision of continental American reach.

memes.label: The Atlantic
memes.description: The Atlantic Ocean in Leaves of Grass is the threshold between Old World and New — the sea crossed by immigrants and ideas, connecting America to its European origins.

memes.label: The City
memes.description: The city in Leaves of Grass as democratic gathering place — teeming, diverse, and sacred in its ordinary life.

memes.label: The Open Road
memes.description: The Open Road in Leaves of Grass is the path of freedom, journey, and self-discovery — both a literal highway and a spiritual condition of endless becoming.

memes.label: Sex
memes.description: Sex and sexuality in Leaves of Grass as sacred life-force — not shameful but celebratory, the root of creativity, intimacy, and cosmic connection.

memes.label: The Sleepers — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'The Sleepers' — Whitman's visionary night-journey through all forms of sleep, from peaceful rest to tormented dreams to the sleep of death.

memes.label: The South
memes.description: The South in Leaves of Grass appears through the lens of Civil War and reconciliation — a region of suffering, beauty, and ultimate re-incorporation into Whitman's democratic Union.

memes.label: The West
memes.description: The West in Leaves of Grass is the direction of American destiny — the horizon toward which the nation expands, the source of new states, and the spatial manifestation of democratic possibility.

memes.label: Time
memes.description: Time in Leaves of Grass collapses past, present, and future into an eternal Now — the poet encompasses all ages, and every moment opens onto eternity.

memes.label: To Think of Time — Analysis
memes.description: Analysis of 'To Think of Time' — Whitman's sustained meditation on mortality, the procession of generations, and the meaning of individual death within cosmic time.

memes.label: First Transcontinental Railroad
memes.description: Completed in 1869, the railroad connecting the eastern U.S. rail network to the Pacific coast — a triumph of engineering that Whitman celebrated as a symbol of American unity and global connection.

memes.label: Ulysses S. Grant
memes.description: American general and 18th President (1822–1885), commander of Union forces in the Civil War, whose death is noted in Leaves of Grass as a moment of national significance.

memes.label: Union
memes.description: In Whitman's poetry, Union signifies both the political preservation of the United States and a cosmic principle of interconnectedness — the merging of self with other, body with soul, individual with the All.

memes.label: Virginia
memes.description: A foundational Southern state referenced across 15 corpus passages in Leaves of Grass, embodying the American paradox of birthplace of liberty and heart of slaveholding territory.

memes.label: Voice
memes.description: Whitman's Leaves of Grass is fundamentally a work of voice — the bardic "I" that speaks across generations, the "barbaric yawp," and the many voices of America that the poet channels and amplifies.

memes.label: Walt Whitman
memes.description: Walt Whitman (1819–1892), author of Leaves of Grass — the "kosmos" who made himself the archetypal democratic self and America's poet-prophet.

memes.label: War
memes.description: War in Leaves of Grass — particularly the American Civil War — transforms Whitman's poetry from celebration to witness, introducing themes of suffering, healing, national sacrifice, and democratic trial.

relations.label: Thou Mother with Thy Equal Brood

memes.label: When Lilacs Last in the Dooryard Bloom'd
memes.description: Analysis of Whitman's great elegy for Abraham Lincoln — lilacs, star, and hermit thrush weave a national lament through pastoral and cosmic imagery.

memes.label: Wisconsin
memes.description: A Great Lakes state referenced across 4 corpus passages, appearing in Whitman's opening catalogue and agricultural litanies as part of the northern Midwest's fertile landscape and democratic community.

memes.label: Women
memes.description: Women in Leaves of Grass occupy a distinctive place — Whitman celebrates feminine beauty and strength while envisioning a democratic equality that transcends gender hierarchy, though his representation reflects 19th-century limitations.

memes.label: Workman
memes.description: The Workman in Leaves of Grass represents the dignity of all manual labor — the common working person whom Whitman places at the center of his democratic poetic vision.

memes.label: Youth
memes.description: The condition of young life that Whitman celebrates as a state of vigor, openness, erotic possibility, and democratic promise — a quality he identifies with America itself.
